reasoning: The market value on October 30, 2025 was 7%. Given the knowledge cutoff of November 9, 2025, there are less than two months remaining for a blockbuster film to be released and acknowledged as AI-written or significantly AI-assisted. Any film with a budget of at least $100 million requires years of development, production, and marketing. Furthermore, the WGA strike in 2023 resulted in an agreement that strictly restricts the use of artificial intelligence in writing literary material for WGA-covered projects (which almost all $100M+ films are), lasting through May 2026. Because of the WGA agreement, major studios are prohibited from using AI to write or significantly assist in writing scripts. Given the time constraints and the contractual limitations on Hollywood writers, it is practically impossible for an AI-written blockbuster to be released before the end of 2025 without prior major news coverage. The 7% market value likely reflects illiquidity or long-shot noise rather than genuine probability. Therefore, I'm setting the probability to 3%.
